commit.message:
fix(vertigo-vault): enable ISR on sitemap for dynamic activity URLs (#24)

* fix(vertigo-vault): use correct column name in sitemap query

vault_activities_cache uses `synced_at` not `updated_at`. The wrong
column name caused the dynamic activity URLs to silently fail (caught
by the try/catch), so the sitemap only contained 4 static routes
instead of all 72+ activity pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(vertigo-vault): enable ISR on sitemap so dynamic routes appear

The sitemap was prerendered at build time (when no DB is available),
so it only contained 4 static URLs. Adding `revalidate = 3600` tells
Next.js to regenerate the sitemap every hour at runtime, when the DB
connection is available and activity slugs can be fetched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Garrett <garrett@windedvertigo.com>

commit.message:
feat(m4a): mSPRT + GST sequential testing (milestone 1.16) (#25)

* feat(m4a): implement mSPRT and GST sequential testing (milestone 1.16)

Implement both sequential testing approaches per ADR-004:

- mSPRT (mixture SPRT): Always-valid p-values with arbitrary peeking,
  using normal mixing distribution (Johari et al. 2017)
- GST (Group Sequential Tests): O'Brien-Fleming and Pocock spending
  functions with pre-specified analysis schedules

Golden-file validation:
- 5 mSPRT golden tests validated against scipy to 4 decimal places
- 4 GST boundary tests validated against scipy/R gsDesign
- 3 invariant tests (spending exhaustion, OBF conservative early,
  monotonically decreasing boundaries)

Also fixes duplicate workspace deps (tokio-stream, walkdir).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update status.md for milestone 1.24

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Prophet with statsmodels Holt-Winters exponential smoothing

Prophet requires cmdstan C++ binary which can't compile on 2GB Lightsail.
Holt-Winters gives same trend+seasonality decomposition with zero native
dependencies. Same interface (ProphetPredictor class) so all ensemble
logic works unchanged.

Add Markdown component with react-markdown and typography plugin

Created the missing Markdown component that page.tsx imports,
installed react-markdown and @tailwindcss/typography to properly
render markdown responses with correct spacing between sections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(chunking): IORT — injecter \n avant الفصل pour article detection

Le scraper IORT extrait les textes sans retours à la ligne, tout sur
une seule ligne : "...الفصل 1 ـ...الفصل 2 ـ...الفصل 3 ـ..."
La regex (?:^|\n)(الفصل|Article) ne détectait aucun article → fallback
adaptive → 3 chunks pour 19K chars (ex: قانون أساسي 23/2012).

Fix : pour iort_gov_tn + stratégie article, injecter \n avant chaque
الفصل/Article avant passage au chunker. Résultat attendu : ~30 chunks
au lieu de 3 pour les textes IORT sans saut de ligne.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add clone+multicast and multicast+selector trace tree tests (#116)

Test the two remaining fork-type interactions:

- clone_and_multicast: ingress clones then sets mcast_grp, producing a
  CLONE fork whose "original" branch nests a MULTICAST fork. Verifies
  that fork decisions don't bleed across fork types.

- multicast_selector: multicast where each replica hits an egress action
  selector, producing 3 levels of nesting (MULTICAST → ACTION_SELECTOR).
  Verifies prefix stripping and decision accumulation at depth > 2.

Golden trace tree suite: 10 → 12 tests.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor: Extract TickEvent messages and InputResult dispatch (#498)

* refactor: TickEvent message separation, InputResult dispatch extraction, LayoutContext threading

Three code health improvements from the architecture review:

1. Move TickEvent message formatting from game logic (tick_stages.rs) to
   presentation layer (tick_events.rs). Removes `message: String` from 18
   variants where the message is derivable from structured fields. Static
   messages become unit variants; derived messages format in the UI layer.

2. Extract Time Vault and Chrono Surge dispatch from main.rs into
   input_routing.rs (dispatch_time_vault_action, dispatch_chrono_surge),
   following the existing dispatch_cloud_action pattern. Reduces main.rs
   by ~380 lines with shared helpers for save/commit/reload patterns.

3. Thread LayoutContext parameter to 5 remaining overlay functions
   (draw_quit_confirm, render_title_browser, draw_bug_report_overlay,
   draw_browser_link_modal, draw_time_vault) for future responsive use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tower initiality theorem and reframe uniqueness as initiality

The Adamek chain from the initial object is initial among all M-generated
chains: any process that generates structural levels by iterating M receives
a unique chain morphism from the Adamek chain. This is the load-bearing
uniqueness statement -- every paper claim references only chain objects and
the colimit, so tower initiality suffices. The BV tensor (global functor
uniqueness) is demoted from critical dependency to additional remark.

New files:
- TowerInitiality.lean: adamekChain_initial theorem (0 sorry)
- TowerMorphismDistinct.lean: two-spec collapse = initiality iso (0 sorry)
- DimensionTowerChain.lean: Adamek chain as GeneratedChain (0 sorry)

Also cleans stale sorry comments across existing files and updates
DensityPropagation, CoherentSelfIndexing, MonoidalUniqueness to 0 sorry.

38 files, 0 sorry, 0 custom axioms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Strategy Interaction Matrices to Portfolio tab

Institutional-grade regime interaction analysis with 4 matrices:
- Trade Overlap: reconstructs trade durations via order matching, measures simultaneous position time
- Return Correlation: Pearson correlation of regime-level combined equity curves
- Signal Concurrency: % of entries within 5-min window across regimes
- Diversification Score: composite metric (0.3*overlap + 0.3*|corr| + 0.4*concurrency)

Color-coded thresholds per partner spec (green/amber/red).
Panel auto-hides when <2 regimes loaded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pass --use-ci-registry to bootstrap in deploy action.

The deploy-kolla-ansible action was only passing --use-ci-registry
to postinstall, but not to bootstrap-kolla-ansible. This meant
the globals.yml always contained the CI registry URL, causing
Kolla-Ansible to try authenticating to gitlab.home.stillhq.com
even when images were built locally. Now bootstrap receives the
flag so it can conditionally strip CI registry settings from
globals.yml when not needed.

Prompt: The CI run in Kerbside is failing because it cannot
authenticate to the CI OCI registry, but it shouldn't need to
given we build the container images locally. I think perhaps
we've set the registry to the CI one somewhere we shouldn't
have.

Signed-off-by: Michael Still <mikal@stillhq.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use custom authDomain directly — fixes mobile redirect sign-in

The IIFE guard required VITE_FIREBASE_USE_CUSTOM_AUTH_DOMAIN=true to use
a custom auth domain, silently falling back to firebaseapp.com. This meant
the redirect flow went cross-origin, which mobile Chrome blocks (third-party
storage). Now the env var is used directly — dev uses firebaseapp.com
(from .env), prod uses goodmeasuregiving.org (from Cloudflare env vars),
which is same-origin and goes through the Worker auth proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move bottom safe-area padding to outer container for PWA

The previous approach added paddingBottom to inner scroll content,
which only created space at the end of the scrollable area. Content
at the visible bottom edge was still clipped behind the Home Indicator
on initial load.

Move the padding to the outer flex h-dvh container instead. This
constrains the flex children (main) so the scroll viewport itself
ends above the Home Indicator — no content is ever behind it.

Add online/offline detection with automatic Cerebras/Ollama fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mobile speech-to-text: correct API URL, add auth, auto-fallback

Root causes of mobile speech-to-text failure:
1. Whisper fallback used hardcoded `/api/reading/transcribe` URL instead
   of API_BASE_URL — wrong server on localhost, CORS failures elsewhere
2. Missing Authorization header on Whisper fetch calls
3. No auto-fallback: mobile Chrome has webkitSpeechRecognition but it
   often fails with network/not-allowed errors — now automatically
   switches to Whisper when native API fails
4. Improved MediaRecorder setup with Safari-compatible MIME types
5. Better error messages for permission denied, no mic, and HTTPS issues
6. Fixed OpenAI API key placeholder check (underscores vs hyphens)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: industry-aware stories + practical translation + TTS-safe text

1. Industry vocabulary mapper — adapts buyer personas, analogies, and
   language based on client's industry (EdTech vs B2B manufacturing vs
   SaaS vs D2C etc). No more "procurement teams" for coaching companies.

2. TTS-safe sanitizer — strips .com, .ai, **, *, —, (), /, # from
   story text so voice generation sounds natural, not robotic.

3. Translation prompt overhaul — practical not literal:
   - Technical product terms stay in English (twisting machine, JEE
     coaching, mock test — never translated)
   - Brand/platform names untouched
   - Domain extensions stripped entirely
   - All special characters removed before translation
   - Emphasis on conversational tone over textbook accuracy

4. Both FOMO 01 and FOMO 02 stories updated with industry-aware vocab.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
